label: calls (X calls Y; caller function -> callee function)
gloss: Function X's body contains a call expression that statically and unambiguously resolves to function Y.
notes: Conservative static relation: only calls the pinned extractor can resolve exactly are asserted; unresolvable call expressions assert NOTHING (closed-world over asserted records, no completeness claim). Structural (non-NSM) definition.

definition.definition: calls(X, Y), forward direction = caller -> callee. The body of function X contains at least one ast.Call node whose callee expression statically resolves, by exact-name resolution only (a bare name bound by a same-module top-level function definition, or an attribute access through a same-corpus imported module's top-level function name, or a from-import-bound name of a same-corpus module's top-level function), to function Y. A call expression the resolver cannot resolve exactly asserts nothing. No dynamic dispatch, no method-receiver inference, no similarity of any kind.
